title: Ops runbook + outcome dashboard + maintenance rotation + content review cadence
context: After the deed is shipped, the project must be sustainably maintained. The ops runbook covers deploy, incident response, source re-verification, and pilot support. The outcome dashboard tracks cases resolved, duty/compliance improvements, and guardrail-audit status -- not DAU. A named maintenance rotation owns the platform. A content review cadence ensures legal content is re-verified and re-signed-off when law changes. Expansion to a second jurisdiction follows a documented, expert-gated process.
objective: Establish sustainable post-delivery operations: write the ops runbook, implement the outcome dashboard (civic metrics only), name the maintenance rotation, define the content review cadence (law-change re-verification + re-sign-off), and document the expert-gated second-jurisdiction expansion process.
acceptanceCriteria: Ops runbook covers: deploy steps, incident response, source re-verification workflow, and pilot support contact
Outcome dashboard tracks: cases moved to resolution, duty/compliance improvements, guardrail-audit status -- not engagement metrics (DAU, sessions)
Named maintenance rotation documented (at least one named owner)
Content review cadence defined: law-change re-verification triggers, review intervals per source type, expert re-sign-off requirement for material legal updates
Process for adding a second jurisdiction is documented and requires expert-gating; not started until M6 is stable
output: A document (and any supporting dashboard code) covering the ops runbook, outcome dashboard, maintenance rotation, content review cadence, and expert-gated second-jurisdiction expansion process.
